review_text:
We verbleven in de budget cabines, bij het inchecken vroeg de mevrouw of de geen upgraden wouden. Aangezien het maar voor 1 nacht was, zijn we hier niet op ingegaan.
De budget cabines zijn sterk verouderd en niet onderhouden. We wisten niet dat budget gelijk stond aan een gebrekkige basis hygiëne: de ventilator was helemaal bedekt onder stof, er waren overal spinnenwebben, de keuken (wat ook de slaapkamer was) rook naar sigaretten, de WiFi werkte niet...
De tip die de vrouw aan de receptie ons gaf willen wij ook meegeven aan hen: just keep on trying, and it will work...

I was in 68 i think it was really nice, there's a long bridge just on the lake. There's a cute duck that come around, he's just there, you can pat him maybe feed him? He poops alot lol. I named him dinoduck, I was bored tbh
Alles da, nette Gastgeber, immer wieder.
Great location and accommodation - great hosts. Love Port Campbell

Great place, in the heart of everything.
Great hosts went out of their way to serve us.
Will be coming back again.

Der Port Campbell Holiday Park, geleitet von dem netten, charmanten Patrick Dinneen, welcher uns sehr freundlich empfing und uns hilfsbereit mit jede Menge Tipps zur Seite stand, als wir im Februar auf der Fahrt über die Great Ocean Road dort Quartier bezogen, können wir nur wärmstens empfehlen. Ein idealer Platz um die Great Ocean Road zur erkunden. Der schmucke, kleine Ort Port Campbell, mit einigen guten Restaurants, liegt nur wenige Gehminuten entfernt. Ebenso sind es nur ein paar Schritte bis zu dem kleinen, aber feinen Beach. Für Wanderfreunde führt direkt ein Küsten-Wanderweg am Holiday Park vorbei.

Perfect location and really helpful staff. The team handled things well and offered us either a refund or a room swap when we had an issue, which we appreciated.

Unfortunately, the cabins do need some work. Our first cabin had mould, so we chose to move. The second cabin was fine, but overall the accommodation could definitely use an update.

It sounds like the site is under new management, so hopefully a revamp is on the way—because the location has so many potential.

Fairly well equipped caravan park. New owners seem keen to update services here. Good recreation room and camp kitchen with BBQ and good cooking facilities. The showers are very temperamental. Smart TV in recreation room so make sure you have young children with you to work out how to use it. We didn't.  No pool here but the beach is so close.
Perfect spot with kindness, and clean facilities, you can't ask for anymore than that.

We had a fantastic stay. Management checked us in and were friendly and happy to help. Even recommended the best lookout spots and great with directions. Amenities are awesome, and the cabin was clean and comfortable. The location is perfect. A great stop to take in all the sights.

We stayed for 2 nights passing through, with a caravan absolutely loved it, friendly check in, walking distance to the beach, cafes, restaurants and shops. Short drive to the apostles and sites. We didn’t plan it , but was lucky enough to be here on the crayfest weekend , great music, parade and food.
wir haben hier im Wohnmobil übernachtet....kein Hotel!

Wir waren mit unserem Camper für eine Nacht dort. Duschen und WC sind sauber. Viel mehr haben wir uns garnicht angeschaut, da unser Zeitplan Recht straff ist. Es gibt noch eine Brücke über die man laufen kann, sah toll aus, allerdings hatten wir auch hierfür keine Zeit mehr.
Stayed for two nights exploring the main attractions on the Great Ocean Road. Campground and amenities is a little tired. Friendly staff all round. Thank you 😊
Friendly genuine checking in process by a very kind hardworking couple. Campsite set in a great area for town and walks.

The staff were excellent. We had a payment issue via a 3rd party and they were so accommodating, wanting to ensure that we had a great holiday dispite this issue.It is in a great location, easy walk to restaurants and beach. Also great to use as a base for sightseeing around the area.
not a lot off facility's three toilets three wash basins good position
In my opinion,  the best location on the Great Ocean Rd

Der Platz war relativ voll, aber gut gelegen. Es gibt nur drei Toiletten für Herren und sechs für Frauen. Das ist etwas wenig für den Platz.
Jedoch die Nähe zur Great Ocean Road und zur Stadt ist super.
Die sanitären Anlagen sollten unbedingt erneuert werden!

The campsite is in a prime location – just a short walk to the bay and the restaurants. It is located directly by the river with the suspension bridge.

However, the site is in urgent need of renovation. In the toilets, the flush buttons are missing, and in the showers there are no shelves to place shower gel or toiletries. The cleanliness also leaves much to be desired. I watched the cleaning staff at work: they were using dirty floor mops and cleaning everything within seconds.

When we arrived, the weather was stormy and rainy, so we wanted to wash and tumble-dry our laundry. Unfortunately, 2 out of 4 dryers were not working. They were not even marked as out of order. We had to hang the laundry inside our motorhome and wait for hours for a dryer to become available. Very inconvenient.
Under new management & thoughtful care taken to all visiting & staying.

Service was really nice, the location is perfect for exploring the great ocean road.We were staying in a cabin. The room was clean, but there was mold in the corners and the ceiling above the shower in the bathroom.

I stayed here in 2019 and it was fine. Recently tried to make a booking using my RACT membership which has to be done over phone or email and reception would not give me the same online price as NRMA members. I've decided not to stay in Port Campbell. What a shame because I'd love to support this local community, especially the hotel and brewery which were quite nice... now I'm taking my business elsewhere. Hopefully staff can reflect on their customer service and communication.

They gave my booked site to someone else who just arrived the day before without booking and assigned me a random one. Then rudely asked me for a proof that I booked that specific site. Very disappointed with NRMA Port Campbell and never coming back here again

Spend Australia Day with fam and friends, getting there it’s easy easy access with all the indications and emails that RNMA send prior arrival, it’s a nice place due to the beach being so close walking distance, a few shops around, the place needs a bit of TLC it’s dated, toilets needs to be take care twice a day due to all the volume of ppl in and out it get messy, the camp sites need to be better basically you have to park and camp in the dirt and it gets windy forget it, the rubbish bins happen to be left right in front of our camp sites and the smell wow during summer, the admin place them at the entrance away from all of us and kids, needs to be more signs of keep quiet after 10 pm at least ppl opening and losing doors and talking loudly until midnight then you get the early birds (people) that can’t sleep and same same, no swimming pool tho
Overall good but needs a lot of TLC
The worst ever Holiday Park!  Be carefull.

What a wonderful stay we had at NRMA Port Campbell Holiday Park The location is superb — the town is super cute, and there’s a lovely walk just outside the park with views over the town, beach, and ocean. The amenities are secure, well maintained, and very clean, making our stay comfortable and worry-free. Everything about this park made for a relaxing, quiet and enjoyable visit. We would definitely recommend it and would stay again!

Put us on a tiny site not suitable for a caravan despite booking for a caravan sized site. Almost impossible to get it, took over 1.5 hours to get in. Sites are tiny, ours was terribly uneven and dusty. If you book go for premium.
Absolute Abzocke. 83 Dollar für eine Person und ein Auto. Die Dame am Empfang ist absolut unhöflich und unverschämt. Bin direkt wieder gefahren, das ist das Geld bei weitem nicht wert.

We love the location and been coming here for over 30 yrs. Sadly this park is at its worst in years. Virtually no grass on the grassed sites advertised. More like the Gobi desert. Yet nearby farmers are haing a bumper year, so rains not the problem. Advertised free wifi is practically non existant. Clean and amenities upkeep is really poor. We have stayed in over 12 NRMA parks, all great. NRMAs worst park ever, its like theve forgotten it exists NRMA should be ashamed of themselves and it. This park has had virtually no improvements in 10 yrs. In fact it keeps getting worse. We come for the location and surrounding friends. Otherwise we would  be out of here. Be prepared!

We holiday here each Christmas and always hire a cabin. The park is situated 5 minutes walk to the beach and the shops. Management is very friendly and the facilities are adequite. Port Campbell is a fishing paradise however the water is too cold for swimming, even in the summer.

Do yourself a favour and avoid this park if possible. Great location but that is all it has going for|It. What was obviously a nice park has fallen into disrepair by seemingly disinterest staff. On the surface the amenities look ok, but by way of example inside the men’s room, the urinal was blocked leaking urine on the floor for the duration of my stay. Some of the locks on shower doors non functional and overall dirty. Never saw a cleaner once. TV in the common room did not work and overall common areas poorly maintained. Premium sites are a must, if you do come, other sites are below par, no grass as stated, think more dry caked mud. There was a sign  explaining they were in drought, but the rest of the area outside the park seems green and lush to me, so I expect equal lack of care. If you do get a premium sight, expect leaves and dirt on the Astro turf, there seems to be no garden maintenance either. Plus sites smallish and quite close with no screening to provide privacy. Ok I guess as long as you like witnessing your neighbours plucking their nose hairs or doing whatever they do. Coming to the end of an eight month trip, have stayed in some great parks, unfortunately this is not one of them.

Poor customer service with booking. Online it shows as available but when I called Kim (customer service)  asked for my email address. She said she will email me so I can reply her email with my enquiry via email. All I needed to know if the sites were available. After an hour I didn’t receive email, I called back she said they had several river front sites available (as did their website). She put me on hold and after a while she got back to me and said sorry her manager says no riverfront sites are available.

Very poor customer service and communication.

2/12/2025

Customer service inconsistent with online about availability. Also online it doesn’t give you the option to choose sites.
Now you are asking to book them online. I just checked again and you’re telling me to book it while you know it’s not available?

What happens when I turn up and there are already people on that site???

Your customer service went from bad to worse!

Every family knows that is a terrible experience on a holiday, when your booking is stuffed with.

Stop your deceiving act and own your mistakes. I can have you reported.

Room was clean, but bathroom grouting in the shower was dirty. King Bed was great, very comfortable. Could do with a lager bench space and microwave in unit would have been nice. However there were 2  in camp kitchen. Staff friendly. We stayed 2 nights. Walking distance to shops was handy.
Location was brilliant. No staff interaction as booked and checked in online the process was simple. The site and facilities need major maintenance and upgrades.

We stayed at NRMA Port Campbell for two nights on our trip from Brisbane to Adelaide. Booking and Checkin was perfect. When we came in trouble with the kettle of our Campervan, Liam saved us. Great Job.
Kay

If it wasn’t for the great location and our pre-paid booking, we would have driven straight out. The park needs significant maintenance: powered sites have poor drainage, and the amenities are old with loose toilet seats and broken cistern buttons. Thankfully, our caravan had its own shower.

The biggest disappointment was the reception staff. We were spoken to rudely on arrival and repeatedly told to “check your email” for all details, even when we simply asked where our site and access codes were. It felt dismissive and unprofessional.

The saving grace is the location—only a short walk to town, cafes and the beach, and a perfect base for visiting the Great Ocean Road attractions. Our site was also spacious with decent drainage.
cozy vibe, nice view, helpful and friendly staff

Camp sites not maintained. No activities for the kids.
Great town and close to the main street and beach
The place is a camping area you can go to the scenic lookout to see the whole town of Port Campbell. The cabin is nice with clean and have all the things you will need.

I only had an overnight stay in a powered site, thank God it was only 1 night. Couldn’t wait to get out of there quick enough. The receptionist was kind enough to moved me to a different site upon check in to be closed to amenities, had I known he was going to assigned us at a corner with a slight incline I would have rescinded the request. We were on a slight incline, we had trouble levelling our motorhome. It became an unpleasant night. I would have understood if the park was fully booked and overnight stay was demoted to a less satisfactory site but it wasn’t. I will attached some photos taken from where my site was, the grey building is the amenities block and there were numerous empty sites from the day before. I still can’t fathom what the motive was behind my designation,

Great location close to shops and places to eat
Reception was rude, unhelpful
Bathroom shower had a lot of mould didn’t look like it had been cleaned for long time
Green mould out of shower
Toilet seat peeling and old
Very disappointed
Rabbits were cute

Camp site tidy, very short walk into town, bridge to the inlet was a lovely feature we hadn’t known about. Amenities good, shower was powerful and warm water. Overall very nice campsite. Grass site for our motorhome was fine but if the ground had been wetter would have been a bit of an issue to get onto the site.

The room was quite dirty with a lot of dust, and the floor was very grubby. I've stayed at two NRMA locations: Port Campbell and Phillip Island, but the room at Phillip Island was much cleaner.

Port Campbell was the first place I stayed, and I first thought maybe it was dirty because the accommodation is a camp site (for caravans/RVs), and perhaps the hotel rooms wouldn't be as clean. But that wasn't true. The NRMA Phillip Island is also a camp site, but their rooms were much cleaner. The floor here was very dirty. The kitchenware was also not very clean and quite old. There were many black ants in the room, and I'm not sure where they came from since we had just arrived and hadn't cooked yet.

The location is good, and the price is affordable
Nice cabins, very comfortable beds, great location,  easy walk into town.  The communal kitchen was an eve opener.

The river with the bridge was nice but it wasn't a great campsite and needs to just have a general updo. We booked a premium campsite but there was nothing premium about it, it was fake and HARD grass that my partner and I had to use a frying pan to put in the tent pegs.

Half decent camp, we booked last minute. When we arrived the office was closed but the receptionist was nice enough to help us get in after hours. Not a huge fan of the amenities. One of the urinals was out order and so was one of the washing machines. Not what I came ye expect from an NRMA tv park

Clean hot showers just pay $5 dollars at the reception or leave the money under the door
Good water pressure
Best option in town if you are traveling

Awesome location  everything was within each other,  the beach, shops , takeaways , look outs , pubs and parks.  Amazing small town for a nice little get away.  Sea sounds were so relaxing.  Definitely worth the stay if you have a caravan

The heating mode of the air conditioner turned off at night and the room temperature dropped. Luckily we found a heater in a cupboard. The toilet is clean with very hot water. Its good for a short stay though. We can see many rabbits outdoor.

Wir haben diesen Campingplatz als Startpunkt für unsere Great Ocean Road Tour gewählt. Wir sind aus Melbourne angereist und waren entsprechend spät angekommen. War aber überhaupt kein Problem, alle notwendigen Informationen (PINs, Stellplatznummer etc) kamen vorab per Mail.
Die Sanitären Einrichtungen waren sauber und aufgrund des kalten und regnerischen Wetters sogar beheizt, was sehr angenehm war! Unser Stellplatz war direkt am Fluss mit Blick auf die Brücke.

Great friendly service. It’s a short walk to lots of dining options which is super handy. Oh, and there are wild rabbits everywhere that are super cute!
We had great service and the room was nice and clean. Super happy!

It is very close to the trail and the bay, just a 5mins walking distance. You can also do trekking to great ocean road Twelve Apostles for 10km, very chilling weather. The room is very adequate facilities & full equipped only thing is that the heater is not working well

Unfortunately we got one of the 2 wettest windy days in the year … Had to dash with our dinner after heating it up back to the room to cold to sit outside Couldn’t dry everything out in the room & got full of condensation Bathroom very draughty & mould around The bed though was surprisingly comfy

We got a villa on site. The unit is quiet, clean and tidy. There’s 2 rooms in the villa comes with a kitchen with all the kitchen tools, a living room and bathroom. Free parking is available.

There’s a lot of cute rabbits on site. You can see them jumping around everywhere in the morning and night.

Sky full of stars. If the weather is good, you might be able to see Milky Way.

Absolutely loved this place!! Highly recommend staying here. Amanda you were fabulous when you booked us in and gave us the perfect campsite for our swags. You gave my partner and friend a list of ideas of where to go and see and your customer service was just excellent! Thank you so much and we will see you again in the near future

Loved our 1 night stay here, we had a powered site and couldn’t decide what camp site we wanted and Amanda at reception was so nice and let us walk around and check out what one we wanted to stay at. Amanda was so nice and accommodating, giving us recommendations for when we arrive in Melbourne. The park is really well looked after and you can even see little bunnies hopping around!

I had a great experience. The room was tidy with everything you expect. I made an error in my booking and Janet was able to assist me in fixing it, great service. The manager Amanda was also lovely to interact with.
It was really good

Amazing place to stay!
My partner and I were on our way from Adelaide planning to do the Grate Ocean road when our car motor blew up as we got to the caravan park on the Wednesday. The lady at the front desk when we checked in was lovely and pointed us in the right direction for a mechanic. We were then told a tow couldn’t get us until Friday morning. They then told us we could add on an extra night at discounted rate and if we needed a late check out they would help arrange. Such a lovely place, our studio cabin was really clean and comfortable, BBQ areas were clean and staff were lovely. Town is close by and so many walks to explore. 110% will be back!

A Beautiful Stay by the Beach!

I stayed one night at NRMA Port Campbell Holiday Park with a caravan and absolutely loved it! The location is perfect, just a short walk to the beach, which made for a peaceful and relaxing atmosphere. The surroundings were beautiful and very well-maintained, with a great natural vibe. It cost only $41 with power.

The park itself was clean and tidy, including the amenities. Everything felt fresh and well-kept. It’s definitely a great spot to unwind, especially if you’re doing a road trip along the Great Ocean Road.

Highly recommended for anyone looking for a scenic, quiet, and convenient place to stay in Port Campbell!
地方十分大，有廳有開放式廚房，一間雙人床房（有暖墊）和一間兩張單人床房，地方十分乾淨，門口就是停車場，十分方便。

Our experience at the NRMA Port Campbell Holiday Park was positioned below average.

The rooms were unclean, particularly the dirty bedsheets that you had to set onto the bed yourself, and many remains of dust and past guests’ hair and dirt were on the bed. Couches has strange stains on it and the floor was suspiciously dirty.

The bathroom was worse. There were hairs everywhere, in the sink and on the ceiling too, alongside spiderwebs and spiders. The shower remote thing did not turn properly and took a while to learn how to use. The sink was tiny and the toilet was dirty.

In terms of amenities, it was average. However they only supplied us with 1 bottle each of Shampoo, Conditioner and Body Wash. The TV had Netflix, Youtube, Prime Video and more, which was great however. The TV could be improved by having a bigger screen, as the show or movie would have a strange black frame surrounded by it and the remote had suspicious stains once again.

The view would’ve been the best part. Our room looked out to a wheat farm that looked gorgeous in the mornings and at sunset. Rabbits also enjoyed lounging in front of our balcony in the mountainous.

The location was average, there were a few restaurants and cafes + healthcare facilities and good liveability factors in the area, however the food quality in these restaurants was not great and food was generally overpriced. Fuel was quite cheap though.

Service: N/A as we met NO staff

Overall our stay could’ve been a lot worse, but the enjoyable view and decent location made it go from horrible to just a little bit below average, along with the general vibe of the holiday park. I also cut it a little bit of slack as it was sponsored by NRMA who helps people, which I love.

An extremely good park in a brilliant location. Close to the main street, bush walks and the beach. Clean modern amenities. Only bad thing is the ridiculously aggressive speed humps.  Very hard with an AMT gearbox, and too much for motorhomes and caravans cabinets and gear.  Not everyone has a 4wd car and van. Very reasonably priced as well, and friendly staff.
近十二門徒石，晚上大部份食店沒有開，有一間便利店，近坎貝爾港，景色優美，房間不大，只睡一晚，尚可，有很多兔子
由於晚了到旅館 所以一早就收到電郵通知如果晚了到達 鎖匙就會放信箱 這點十分細心 所以都沒有享受什麼服務 但checkout 時也十分快捷及有禮這間旅館要開大約5分鐘車程可到附近的小型超市晚上十分寒冷 但有暖氣及顫提供 在房間外的草地 有數隻很可愛的兔子另外有共用廚房可以使用最後離開前要自行清走垃圾
good location to twelve Apostles. Clean and good
They need to update their opening hours. They say they close at 6, but stop answering phones before 5 and close up. Even the sign on their reception door was wrong.

Lovely park beautiful relaxing and enjoyable. However needs a bit of money spent to repair rotten wood staircase and to camp kitchen and either rocks or synthetic grasse put down as middle sights were just mud.
It's always a pleasant experience staying here. The facilities are great, staff friendly and rooms comfortable. My 2nd time coming here in 3 years. We even did a little Aussie BBQ! Fantastic!

This is probably my favorite campground so far. We were staying in a tent, so we relied heavily on the facilities and really didn’t want to be constantly walking through mud and wet grass — especially when it rains.

The staff actually asked us at check-in if we wanted to stay closer to the amenities, which meant a lot to me. After staying at so many different campgrounds, I’ve realized that most places don’t really care about whether guests have those kinds of needs, let alone offer to help.

The facilities here are great — the bathrooms even have heat lamps, which was such a thoughtful touch! The whole campground is clean and clearly well maintained on a daily basis. If I ever come back, I’d definitely stay here again.
Spacious room area

We had a 5 night stay in the caravan park. We had a great campsite and Jake and Amanda are great hosts.Janet at reception was very helpful and all the staff, Kassidy , were very friendly and knowledgeable about the area.
The location is perfect, walking distance to the town centre and we had the best night at the local, such a great welcoming little pub with great food.
Thanks everyone and we hope to be back soon.

Great camp for us as we were in motorhome…. We got a nice spot against river, was bit tight on reversing as lots of large vehicles but we managed 😜
Camp kitchen was lovely and clean as were the toilets/showers.
It was perfectly located for visiting some of the great ocean road sites especially to catch a sun set at the apostles
Great place to be, amazing facility couldn’t ask for more
Great caravan park. We had a site near the creek which was lovely.

I stayed in a Studio Cabin for one night after spending 6 days hiking the Great Ocean Walk.  Room had everything I needed including a comfy bed and a good shower with lots of hot water and excellent pressure.

Location of the park is good.  I didn't use any of the facilities.

We had a powered site near the entry which are up a big hump for some reason. Was very difficult to straighten a big van without scraping.
The amenities were a little crowded too.
We did love the bridge over the creek and the look ittle walk at the top of the stairs .

We spent a couple of nights in a cabin recently. The park is in a perfect location , walking distance to shops, restaurants, pub and is right on the water.|Amenities are great with communal cooking room with stove tops, bbqs and fridges and freezers.|All staff are super helpful and friendly.

People are 10/10 but cabin wasn't perhaps suitable to our needs.

Shower was broken on arrival and we didn't find out until after staff hours when I went to shower. Head fell off the wall and the entire bathroom got sprayed. Used some of our spare towels to clean the water and found the floor all over was quite dirty. Rang reception the next day in business hours and a lovely guy (?Jake) came to fix it soon after. Getting partially refunded for the first night for this but still yet to recieve it.

Sink had VERY intermittent hot water. I think when everyone down the hill showers the hot water runs out quickly because I spent a lot of the time boiling a kettle just to wash bottles and pump parts for our newborn. Time consuming and annoying. Fridge also missing a door shelf and had a completely frozen over freezer section. Had to rearrange the internal shelves and remove one to fit our things in.

Double bed has seen better days, laying on it feels like you're tilted head down and it was quite uncomfortable. By day 3 we were both very sore and grumpy (as if getting up 700 times to a newborn during the night doesn't already make you grumpy. 2 year old slept in a portacot but did sit on the single beds during the day to read books or play - didn't love that one of the beds was under a window with a blind cord 10cms above the mattress height - massive strangulation hazard and as a nurse I've seen too many near misses to be comfortable with her sitting there unsupervised. - Hard when you have a newborn who also needs you and the 2 year old can climb into the couch and from there onto the bed when you aren't looking. Had to resort to putting her in the portacot anytime I had to attend to her sister and my husband wasn't around - she was none pleased.

Rabbits around the place are ADORABLE. 2 year old was a big fan and has talked about it ever since.

Walk into town was super close, well situated with great views just a short walk away. Gate behind the cabins is locked though so to get there you have to walk down a gravel driveway and back up a path with a hill. Would have been convenient to use the gate, especially with a double pram and very undesirable weather.

Finally, on check out I arrived at the office at 9.45 am after bustling two crying, grumpy children into car seats and 700 bags of luggage tetris style into the boot to find the place unattended. Stood there for 10-15 mins waiting whilst my husband stayed in the car listening to the children scream (both settled as soon as we started driving but didn't like the hold up). Finally had someone attend to me who said she was out training new staff - understandable sure but if check out time is 10 am, surely you could either ensure someone is at the desk until 10 am or you could have a doorbell to push to alert staff someone is waiting. I was probably a little curt to the poor kind girl but we had a 5 hour drive ahead of us with 60 microstops along the way to resettle/feed/excerise the kids and the 15 min wait was frustrating.

It was central to Port Campbell. Air-con wasn't working (30+). Provided with portable air-con. Very noisy & hard to sleep. Fan was unbalanced & wobbly. Freezer in bar fridge frozen solid & unusable. Was expecting better.
Wir waren im März 2025 hier. Wir hatten eine Basic-Ferienhütte für 3 Tage. Kleine und bescheidene Hütte. Einfach eingerichtet. Für bescheidene Ansprüche ausreichend.
Great place and was made better with no Rainy Weekend.
Nice spot.Jake was super helpful
Excellent park, close to town and a really amazing beach. A great pub at the top of the road, what more do you need 😁 park clean and very tidy...hosts are friendly and helpful.

Room and Service do not apply to this location if one is occupying a camp site. The overall quality of facilities and their upkeep could be improved a bit. The management must enforce the rules esp. as they relate to pitching the tents.

Added after response from Holiday Park owner:
Re: the rules enforcement, there was a group of at least 3-4 tents pitched too close together right across from the toilets area in a very unsafe manner, with a lot of electrical lights and equipment inside causing potential fire hazard, with multiple cars parked adjacent to and all around them disregarding the parking spots. This went on for at least 48 hours. This shouldn't have been permitted by the management staff.
Pretty average. Nothing for kids. Sites are all pretty much mud/dirt/gravel where there is no mats. Good location but the park itself needs work.

Really, the only thing this caravan park has going for it is the proximity to the beach. Otherwise dont let the photos of the park fool you. It's old, poorly maintained, and the amenities are incredibly dirty and they smell. The signs say that the amenities are cleaned daily, but the dirty pink loofa that's been sitting in one corner of the ladies amenities for three days, suggests otherwise. If you can, consider the rec ground nearby as an alternative to this place.

We got a camp-site for our swag as we were travelling the great ocean road on our bikes.

Issues:
1) pictures look much more serene than how it currently presents, you get a patch of rocky dirt. We didn't even get fake grass.
2) we were situated directly in front of the amenities, the sound of the hand dryer was so damn loud. We had to get up early, it felt like one after another the hand dryer wouldn't stop till at least midnight.
3) we wanted to freeze some of our water bottles to keep our food cold. Camp kitchen doesn't open till 7am.
4) amenities were not too bad, but had no where to actually sit your things down to get dressed where the floor isn't wet from showers.

Beaucoup trop bruyant. Personne ne respecte le couvre feu imposé. Et personne n’est là pour le faire respecter. Le séchoir à mains des toilettes s’entend de l’autre côté du parc. Passez votre chemin pour une nuit tranquille.

Booked a 'premium' powered site with synthetic grass, advertised as suitable for tents. This turned out to be one of the worst experiences I’ve ever had. Spent nearly an hour trying to get just three pegs in barely, as the site was full of gravel and solid underneath. Desperate for help, we called the emergency contact number several times, but no one answered. What’s the point of having an emergency contact number if it’s never picked up? To make matters worse, we were given a sloped site. How are we supposed to sleep in a tent on a slope like that? We provided all the necessary information when booking, so they knew exactly what we needed. They should have assigned us a better site, especially since plenty were available. The toilet facilities were also dark, dirty, and smelled awful. In the end, we gave up, left disappointed and wasted $107. Never again

By god welling we be back next year
The area it beautiful close to all shops restaurants I enjoy my 3 nights
I love the rabbits.....
The place it very clean
I met guy from Canada he work there his very good........for me everything very good excellent

Wir haben den Campingplatz spontan gebucht und sind super zufrieden. Gute Anbindung zu den 12 Aposteln. Duschen und Toiletten sehr sauber. Die Dame mit der ich an der Rezeption gesprochen habe war super freundlich.
Ein Campingplatz des NRMA, ordentlich und sauber, die Umgebung ist ganz nett.
It appears to need some tender loving care to bring it up to a better standard.  Stayed on a powered site. Staff were great and very helpful.  Location looked tired and needing an upgrade.

I’ve done two nights camping with my family. The location is great, friendly staff. The shared toilets, shared kitchen maintained well. There are plenty of flies around the area (the whole 12 apostles and great ocean drive). Overall, we enjoyed our stay and may comeback again.

Great location right on the beach with a small creek with a beach suitable for kids
Often booked out at peak times
Walking distance to the town area which has a great selection of shops and restaurants
There is a brew pub over the road and the pub is a few minutes walk
The beach is about 3 minutes from the furthest part of the park
The cabins are nice and the campground is also nice
Have to have security numbers, passwords etc just to get in and out, plus it's all on again just to go toilet. Crazy and way over the top.

Stayed for 3 nights in our caravan. lovely staff that are very helpful. Clean amenities and plenty of room. Great location with stunning views. Walking distance to beach and shops. Would stay again in a heartbeat.
Nice holiday park with really good facilities. Great camp kitchen and recreation area. Right next to the centre of town and the beach

Service was fantastic, Both Jake and Amanda are very helpful in making your stay an enjoyable one -  the booking and late check-in instructions so easy to follow and information about local birds chirping around.
Our cabin's location is lovely - really feel the serenity.  The room is clean but its missing a table.
Over all our stay has been a delight apart from our last night when  bugs attracted to the light outside the door  unfortunately  creep inside the room!😟

Good location right in front of the beach and there’s a bridge out the back of the campsite where you can have a nice walk with a beautiful view of the town. They have a good kitchen (bring your own cooking stuff), good bathrooms, huge TV and also a gym.

Loved our stay in a Villa and would highly recommend it. Photos don't do it justice. Location was perfect and it was quite even though the park is close to town. Clean, comfy and there was a coffee machine and airfryer!
Not too bad 😹

Fabulous park - great location and super friendly owners
Lovely big sites
Loved port Campbell
Very nice
A good little park, we opted for the waterfront spot which had some nice views of the river. Amenities clean. Located in the centre of town, would recommend.
Ottima camera in un bellissimo posto in mezzo alla natura e...al fiume straripato per tre giorni di pioggia continua.

I like the location especially when I travelled from the another side of the great ocean road (it is quite a good pit stop to rest after 3 hours ride from Melbourne city, and visited a few beaches before getting back to NRMA park).
My concern was the cabin was full of spider web, the floor was a little dusty too.

호주 여행 중 최고의 기억이었습니다. 날씨가 잘 맞은 것도 있지만 여기에서 본 은하수는 잊지 못할 것 같아요. 시설이 호텔처럼 깔끔하지는 않지만 지내기에 전혀 불편함 없었습니다.
8월 기준 밤 온도가 낮아 쌀쌀하기도 하지만 전기장판이 있어서 잠은 따뜻하게 푹 잤습니다.
이 곳에 대한 기억이 너무 좋아서 나중에 꼭 다시 가보고 싶어요. 캠핑장으로도 한번 가보고 싶네요.

We had an enjoyable stay in a creek side villa ( no 17). The villa is what you would expect in a park and for the value price.  All the basic facilities and very clean. Very friendly owners made check in great and provided plenty of info on exploring the local attractions (12 apostles etc).  we would stay again
It’s an economical place. Clean rooms and bathrooms. Close to restaurants and grocery stores…

This is a really great park, we had site next to river. it is a quiet location and easy access to the town. we were  here in June, so a little cold, it was really nice to have heated and very clean amenities. Really had a lovely stay here, will definitely recommend this park.

Great park. We arrived late so called ahead, and the staff were happy to help. The after hours check in was easy, and the little smiley face on our envelope was a lovely touch to the end of a long day. Thoroughly enjoyed the walk up to the point which is accessible from the park. Great facilities, lovely staff, very happy. Thank you :-)

Very reasonable priced caravan park right next to the big attractions on the Great Ocean Road.
Bathrooms very clean and tidy with heating!
Would definetely recommend this park.

We stayed in a cabin.  The place takes a while to heat up. The queen bed has bed warmers. Dining area is spacious, with fully equipped kitchen.  Easy check in and out, with information provided if u miss the reception hours.||Very comfortable and clean.

Power camp site cost Aud40. The camp site is equal to its price. They place us far away from the toilet. We not even use its kitchen and bathroom. The walkway is muddy. We move out the next day early morning to other camp site along great ocean road. No comment at all.

Parked our van in this great park whilst we explored the Great Ocean Road. Big sites, clean ( and heated ) amenities. Friendly reception staff. Easy walk to town , not so easy walk up the steps at the back lol, but worth the view
Nice place, easy check in, helpful staff, lotsa nature w wildlife around, great walk to the suspension bridge and to the beach.
NRMA Park it's much cheaper than G'DAY parks in the area and it's also much better.
We have stayed here before. The park is small and  tidy. Amenities are clean. Wifi signal is strong. The synthetic grass site is quite neat for swag.

If you do the Great Ocean Road. This stop is a must. The van sites are great and the studio cabins have king size beds. Grey water has to run on the ground on the sites near the river. Not sure about the others. Well maintained and very clean. Lots of rabits st night.
Nice small town I always go with NRMA

It is a really good caravan park. It is close the Great Ocean Road tourist attractions. The little township of Port Campbell is right at your door. Has plenty of cafes and eateries. There is a Friendly Grocer for all your needs, a pub, a brewery (on The Artisan Food trail).

Travelled with Tesla Model Y. Location is not yet EV compatible, but NRMA is catching up on EV charging. So hopefully in future should have compatible infrastructure.
1 Star less as the 15amp plug is tilted towards inside and I couldn’t fix my Tesla Wall charge. So pls travel with appropriate adapter. (Photo attached)
Else beautiful location, beach is easily accessible and all key locations are within 30mins of driving.
Not too many things to do within, but encourages kids to explore nature which is in plenty.
Amenities clean, but pls travel with cutlery as they are not present in communal kitchen.(pots and pans available) Bathroom and toilets clean. Staff lovely and welcoming.
Awsome staff. Very friendly and helpful staff

If you want a decent place with walking distance to the beach and want a break away from your phone/wifi etc, this is the place to be at. Location is great (walking distance to beach and all eateries) and rates are affordable (as long as you book in advance). You can go caravan style and get your trailers or take up a cabin or suites if you don't want the adventure. Either way this place caters to you fine.

Good location, short walking distance to town, toilet is generally clean, tap water pressure/heat is good, shower water pressure is good, kitchen is well equipped for washing and water pressure is good too, wifi speed can be improved considering that some travellers may be working on the go or remotely.

Had a powered site for the van. Staff were so lovely, amenities were cleaned daily. Located right in the Main Street and on a lovely river
Only down was the parking was a bit tight
조용하고 멋진 바닷가 작은마을

Der Campingplatz ist sehr zentral gelegen. Alle Highlights an Great Ocean Road kann man gut und relativ schnell erreichen. Das Visitorcenter ist gleich beim Campingplatz. Die Sanitäranlagen sind sehr sauber und gepflegt. Allerdings bei Wind kann es sehr staubig werden. Das war für einen Stadt Campingplatz ungewöhnlich.
Eine tolle super saubere Kabine. Sehr schöner ruhiger Platz mit Zugang zum Meer. Viele schöne Pubs und Cafés in der Nähe

My partner and I had a great experience camping here. Safe, quiet and good facilities. Great location.

Affordable also.

3 stars for proximity to town/beach; facilities and general park layout and ambience. Where you lost 2 stars is advertising (and charging accordingly) for ‘grass’ sites that clearly aren’t (nothing quite like that blue metal dust through everything with even only the slightest breeze) and the bunting. What’s the go with the circus style/traffic hazard/building site bunting?!? Not only is it unsightly, but it’s not actually separating anything of note or denying access (as evidenced by the myriad of folks stepping over, under and through it!!). Unless you’ve had some type of recent gray nomad turf battle that warrants its retention to define van sites, it really should go!!
Camped one night. Easy sites for a motorcycle. Right by the beach. Nice cafes and restaurants nearby. Gorgeous little beach town.

Excellent accomodation . We are travelling from Melbourne to Adelaide on a road trip and we caravan in GB so we know a good caravan site when we see one , and this is.  We stayed in a one room apartment with iron , hairdryer kitchenette etc all very good. Small bathroom but very clean and with a good shower . TV and digital services,  I'll never complain about my TV at home again. Single beds , but very comfortable and no need for the AC all-night.  There is an outdoor covered kitchen area with seating for twelve across the way,  with microwave , double electric BBQ and sinks and a large communal fridge. We used it both evenings as we shopped locally in the only supermarket. There is also a laundry room with several machines and room to hang out your clothes in the sun to dry. There were plenty of restaurants to choose from if you wished.  Some good walks nearby , with challenging sets of steps.  The beach was a stones throw away and really nice. Gentle lapping waves , good expanse of sand and a wall to sit against.  Good location for all the visitor attractions along the coast.  Highly recommended if you don't want 5* hotel accomodation.

Toller ordentlicher Platz direkt am Meer und Fluss.
Hatten eine ruhige Nacht.
Im nahen Umfeld Cafe/Restaurant vorhanden.

Great place. Location is awesome. Just a 3 minute walk to the beach. Service was excellent and very friendly. Management were quite laid back, easy going and very accommodating. Camp sites are great. They have synthetic grass on a number of sites. This looks good, keeps the dust down and makes for a nice surface if walking around barefoot after the beach. The grass can get a little hot though.
Also plenty of little visitors come by. (Rabbits & little blue birds)
Would definitely recommend staying here and will definitely be going back.
Note, not a hotel, but a caravan park. There are fully self contained cabins on site too.

The park itself was lovely  an well kepted.
Very disappointed in there booking system  especially  when you booked it 6 months b4 an paid, thinking that we were booked in with our friends  only to find we were shafted to the other end.
And then we had to leave  our van there for a emergency at home. But still had to pay for site as our van was on it.
They stuffed up our booking from the start would have thought they could have credited us only to be told no.
Extremely disappointed
Won't return
Will not recommend.

The location is great but the Holiday Park itself is very basic with not enough amenities for the campsite that big, the toilets are constantly dirty; no playground for the kids and I wouldn’t stay here the second time and wouldn’t recommend it to my friends
Quiet little area in Port Campbell. Clean and nice.

We stayed 4 nights in a studio cabin, the bed was so comfortable and the cabin was clean and had everything we needed.
The park people are very helpful and friendly 😊
Great location, nice staff

Quiet place in the heart of Port Campbell, the creekside cabin had a great view and well maintained.
The bed in the bedroom had heating pads which were great!
So peaceful staff very helpful and clean rooms it's a +++ for us.
Great location for a short or long stay, So close to the beach and good facilities. Friendly staff too
Camping très bien placé, emplacement très correct, sanitaires très propres et calme assuré accueil très sympathique et le propriétaire fera tout pour vous aider en cas de problème.N’hésitez pas

Quiet location next to River, walk to beach. Would have been better if they had a microwave in the studios. The King Studio was large, clean, and tidy. The Sow and Piglets brewery pub and cafe are across the road from the entrance to this park. The main street and shops are around the corner and are a very easy walk.
I camped here. The location is nice with sea, river and mountains all together. It's no drone zone so not allow to fly. 10 min from 12 Apostle. Worth coming to stay here.

We only stopped one night, I wish we had stayed longer. The staff were very friendly and helpful. The location is fantastic and the amenities are the best we've come across in the 3 months we've been travelling. The camp kitchen is first class.
Excellent town and camp.
Great service, brilliant price, helpful and welcoming. Comfy beds in cabin. Thanks a bunch Nicky for great service

Fantastic location near the end of the Great Ocean Road. We quite enjoyed our stay here and found the price to be fair. The bathrooms/facilities were OK, which is the only reason why this is a 4 instead of a 5-star review
Right next to the beach but the amenities were full of insects and flies, the rest was great

It was the worst shower I've ever taken. I could not regulate the temperature because it changed any time even though the male shower was not busy. That happened becouse they built the water instalation with really thin pipelines
Absolutely beautiful place. A good base to visit the Great Ocean Road spots from.

Great location, lovely staff, but the most disgustingly dirty camp kitchen 🤢 we were there for 3 nights and it was tidied a couple of times but everything was left filthy. The kettle was filled with mould, crumbs on all the shelves, fridges had old food left that stunk everything out, bugs floating in the dish liquid containers, windows were all oily, floors looked like they'd never been cleaned and it all stunk. Needs a deeeeeeep friggen clean.

Arrived to a friendly welcome and a run down of facilities. We got a premium spot. The site is big and has water and power with room for van artificial grass area and room for car.facilities are fabulous and park was well kept. Very pretty spot close walk to beach and town. We could park the RV and explore on foot. Went to sleep listening to the waves crashing on shore. Lots of rabbits around the park. Only con would be that Port Campbell doesn’t have TV reception but the WIFI was free and good so we could stream
A movie. There is a huge TV in the recreation room for guests. All staff are wonderful. Just a quick walk to a local brewery and pub. Lots of restaurants cafes and local supermarket all in the next street.

Ottima cabin a Port Campbell
Ci siamo fermati qui passando per la Great Ocean Road e siamo rimasti piacevolmente sorpresi da questa cabin nel bosco. Il paesino é molto pittoresco e tutti sono gentili. Siamo arrivati dopo l'orario di chiusura della reception e ci hanno fatto trovare le chiavi. La cabin molto pulita e carina.
Tutto il paesino é popolato da conigli ovunque.
Per informazioni il personale al visitor center é molto gentile e disponibile.
Molto consigliato!
Really enjoyed our stay, my favorite caravan park to date. With the cleanest facilities, best location & accommodating; lovely staff members.
Lovely little park with a lot to offer. Nice short walk  into the township.

Stayed in a creek side villa cabin and it was so lovely. Clean and nicely organised with great appliances. 2 rooms were near and tidy and bathroom was also nice with great water flow. Definitely recommend!
Excellent! Stayed in cabin 17. One of the best cabins I have ever stayed in
Loved it... tidy and clean and staff very friendly.

ที่นี่มีกระต่ายเยอะ ยามกลางคืนมีกระต่าย มาเล่นชมจันทร์ จำนวนมากมาย  ธรรมชาติสวยงามมาก ที่พักติดกับลำธาร มีทางเดิน tracking ขึ้นไปมองวิว ห้องพักที่เป็น Cabin น่าอยู่ อาจจะไม่ดีเลิศ แต่คุ้มค่า พื้นเย็น แต่อุปกรณ์ทำครัว ทุกอย่างครบถ้วน ผ้าห่มสะอาด  มีทางเดินห่างจากทะเลแค่ 100 กว่าเมตร ห่างsupermarket 300ม. และ ใกล้กับร้านเบียร์ The Sow and Piglets อันมีชื่อเสียง และ ใกล้สมาคมเซิร์ฟ

The cabin I stayed in the Shower had no water pressure, the WIFI does not work. Some lights did not work. The TV worked intermittently. Need 2 remote controls just to turn it on.
Very poor standards.
I would not recommend staying here at all.
Loved staying here! We stayed for two nights in a studio cabin and it was neat and comfortable, with lovely staff, and the town itself is a gem! Will be back I’m sure 😊
